title: Formal proof versus simulation sampling
description: Formal verification proves a property over all reachable states, while simulation only samples the input space it drives.
body: Use when choosing between formal and simulation for a given property. Do not expect simulation to prove a universal property, since it can only demonstrate the cases it runs. Actions: reserve formal for exhaustive control properties, use simulation for data-path and system scenarios, and verify formal proofs reach a meaningful bound. Validation signals include a formal proof bound deep enough to be meaningful and simulation coverage filling what formal cannot scale to. Risk boundary: an unbounded formal property may only be proven to a shallow depth, so flag a proof whose bound is too small to cover the real behavior.
